framework.name: Legal Personhood Gap for Autonomous Agents
framework.description: AI agents have no legal identity or personality — only legal persons (individuals or registered organizations) can hold assets and enter contracts. This means any transaction an agent makes is legally the user's/organization's responsibility, but creates ambiguity about enforceability of agent-signed agreements and liability allocation. Currently no legal framework for 'electronic personhood' despite debate.
framework.raw_quote: By law, only legal persons (individuals or registered organizations) can hold assets and enter contracts. An AI agent, no matter how autonomous, is not a legal person; it's more akin to a tool or software service.

source.title: Enabling Autonomous AI Agents to Make Payments - LinkedIn
